title: amborellales
intro: The amborellales rank is a taxonomic order of flowering plants, specifically a distinct group within the angiosperms. It is one of the most ancient lineages of flowering plants, comprising a limited number of species. The amborellales order includes several notable families, such as Amborellaceae, which is the only family within this order, as well as the more widespread families Orchidaceae, Euphorbiaceae, and Cyperaceae, although these latter three are not part of the amborellales order. The amborellales order is characterized by its unique floral structure and reproductive biology, setting it apart from other flowering plant groups.

families.family_desc: Amborellaceae is a family of flowering plants that is considered one of the most ancient lineages of flowering plants, found in Southeast Asia and New Guinea.
